name: Robert Chen
chief_complaint: I keep getting this squeezing in my chest when I climb stairs or walk too fast, and it's been happening more and more this past week
onset_description: Episodes started about 3 weeks ago with exertion but over the past week have been coming on with less and less activity. Today it happened just walking from the parking lot.

chest_pain.description: Squeezing pressure across the chest that comes and goes with activity
chest_pain.character: squeezing, like a tight band around my chest
chest_pain.location: across the front of the chest, more on the left side
chest_pain.radiation: left shoulder and inner left arm
alleviating_factors: stopping and resting for a few minutes

shortness_of_breath.description: Gets winded much easier than usual, especially when the chest squeezing happens
shortness_of_breath.character: feeling like I need to stop and catch my breath

lightheadedness.description: Feels a bit dizzy when the chest pain is at its worst
lightheadedness.character: like the room tilts for a second

fatigue.description: I just don't have the energy I used to, even simple things wipe me out

labs.CBC: WBC 7.8, Hgb 14.6, Plt 230 - within normal limits
labs.BMP: Na 141, K 4.5, Cr 1.0, Glucose 118 (mildly elevated) - BUN 16

social_history.smoking: Current smoker, about half a pack per day for 30 years (15 pack-year history)
social_history.living_situation: Lives with wife and teenage son in two-story house
social_history.exercise: Very little, used to play basketball but stopped about 2 years ago
social_history.diet: Eats out frequently, heavy on red meat and fried foods

conditions.father: Coronary artery disease, had bypass surgery at age 58, Type 2 Diabetes
conditions.mother: Hypertension, stroke at age 70, alive at 79
conditions.paternal_uncle: Died of heart attack at age 50

personality: stoic and matter-of-fact, tends to downplay symptoms and needed convincing from wife to come to the ED
communication_style: Brief and to the point. Answers questions directly without a lot of elaboration. Will say 'it's not that bad' frequently. His wife, who is at bedside, will sometimes add details he leaves out.
pain_description_style: Uses simple, functional descriptions ('it squeezes,' 'it's tight'). Reluctant to rate pain highly. Will underreport if not pressed for specifics.

critical_findings: Progressive exertional chest pain with decreasing activity threshold (crescendo pattern)
Pain radiates to left shoulder and arm
Pain relieved by rest (classic anginal pattern)
Normal initial troponin requiring serial monitoring to rule out evolving MI
Significantly elevated LDL and triglycerides with low HDL
Strong family history of premature coronary artery disease
recommended_interventions: Cardiology consult for possible cardiac catheterization
